Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A train is negotiating a curve of 875 m radius on a broad gauge (BG) track at a speed of 90 km/h. The actual cant provided on the curve is 30 mm. The gauge width for BG track is taken as 1676 mm. Calculate the cant deficiency and state whether it is within the permissible limit as per Indian Railways standards.
Select the correct option.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A car (vehicle A) is travelling behind a slower vehicle (vehicle B) on a two lane two way road.
The design speed of the road is 96 kmph, and the speed of vehicle B is 80 kmph. The acceleration of vehicle A during overtaking is 0.9 m/s2. The space headway maintained before overtaking is 18 m, and after overtaking is 22 m. Assume the reaction time of the driver as 2 seconds. The minimum length of overtaking zone is _____ m. (Rounded off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 1636.41
Solution

1636.41 (1634 - 1638)

 
Q3 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Aerial photographs are taken using a camera with a focal length of 152 mm and size of photograph is 20 cm × 20 cm. The survey is conducted at a scale of 1 : 25000, with 60% forward overlap. If there is a parallax measurement error of 0.12 mm, then estimate the corresponding error in height (in meters) at the photographed point. (Round off your answer to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 5.70
Solution

5.70 (5.65 - 5.75)

 
Q4 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A valley curve of length 131.6 m is being designed on a road where a descending gradient meets an unknown ascending gradient. The headlight of a vehicle is mounted at a height of 0.8m and the beam makes an angle of 1.5° with the horizontal. The headlight sight distance (HSD) is 100 m. The allowable rate of change of acceleration is 0.6 m/s3. If the descending gradient is 4%, then
determine the value of the ascending gradient (in percentage rounded off to nearest integer).

NAT
Correct answer: 5
Solution

5 (5 - 5)

 
Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

During geometric alignment of a roadway, a horizontal curve with a radius of 75 m is introduced.
The ruling gradient for this terrain is 6.0%. Find the grade compensation and the compensated gradient at the curve. If for a certain straight stretch of this road in this terrain, a camber of 2.5% is provided for drainage, calculate the hydraulic gradient.

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(c, d)

 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A vertical tower appears in a vertical aerial photograph taken from a flying height of 950 m above datum. The radial distances of the top and bottom of the tower from the principal point are 123.4 mm and 92 mm respectively. The elevation of the tower’s base is 300 m from MSL. Calculate the height of the tower. (Assume MSL as datum).

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Which of the following is a source of energy detected by a passive sensor in remote sensing?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A car is travelling at 90 km/h on a highway with an ascending gradient of 3%. The perceptionreaction time is 2.5 seconds, the coefficient of friction is 0.35, and the braking efficiency is 80%. The driver applies brakes and reduces speed to 10 m/s before releasing the brakes. Select the option(s) which is/are correct:
(Take g = 9.81 m/sec2)

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, d)

 
 
Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Calculate the extra widening required (in meters, upto 2 decimal places) on a single lane road having a radius of horizontal curve of 70 m. The design speed is 30 km/h and the length of the wheelbase of the vehicle is 6m.

NAT
Correct answer: 0
Solution

0 (0 - 0)
As mentioned in IRC 73 : 2023, Table 6.6, no extra widening is to be provided for radius
greater than 60 m for single lane roads. For two lane roads, it is 300 m.

Q10 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A crest curve is to be designed for a highway where a gradient of 0.8% meets another gradient of – 1.1%. The design speed is 80 kmph. To ensure safe overtaking on this highway, the length of the crest curve should be adequate to provide visibility over a distance of 470 m. The minimum required length of the crest curve will be

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A two lane road is being designed for mixed traffic conditions. If the design speed is 70 kmph and the road has a horizontal curve of radius 180 m, then how much should the outer edges of the pavement be raised (in m) if the pavement is to be rotated with respect to the central line? The total width of the pavement includes width due to extra widening. Assume length of vehicle
as 6 m and normal width of two lane pavement as 7m (Round off to three decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 0.271
Solution

0.271 (0.265 - 0.275)

 
Q12 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A high type bituminous road pavement of 7 m width is to be constructed in a region with heavy rainfall. The camber is parabolic and the allowable cross slope is as per IRC recommendations. If the height of the road surface at a certain point P is 5.5 cm above the edge, then the horizontal distance of point P from its nearest edge is ______ meters (Answer upto two decimal points)

NAT
Correct answer: 1.88
Solution

1.88 (1.85 - 1.95)

 
 
Q13 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

At an airport, the average of the daily maximum temperatures in the hottest month is 50°C, and the average of the daily mean temperatures for the same month is 38°C. What is the reference temperature to be considered for airport design? (Answer in °C, rounded off to nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 42
Solution

42 (42 - 42)

 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A 4° curve branches off from a 2° main line curve in the opposite direction in a B.G. yard layout.
If the speed on the diverging track is limited to 35 kmph, then determine the allowable speed on the main line. (Assume gauge distance = 1.676 m for B.G.)

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A two lane two way road in rolling terrain has a design speed of 60 kmph and a horizontal curve of 240 m radius. The width of the carriageway on straight portion is 7.5 m, and the wheelbase of the vehicle is 6m. As per IRC, the rate of introduction of superelevation for rolling terrain is 1 in 150 and the pavement is rotated about the inner edge. The required length of the transition curve is _____ m. (Answer upto one decimal point)

NAT
Correct answer: 81
Solution

81 (79 - 82)

 
Q16 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A two lane highway with a total carriageway width of 7.5 m has a horizontal curve of radius 220 m and total curve length of 260 m. If the desired sight distance is 360 m, then the required set-back distance from the outer edge of the road is _____.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

 
Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A parabolic vertical curve is being designed to connect two roads, one with a grade of +2% and the later with a grade of – 3%. The length of the curve is 400 m and RL of vertical point of curvature is 600 m above MSL. The difference in RL of highest point on curve and the vertical paint of tangency is _______ .

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
